eval_name: Implement dispatches subagent — never writes code itself
prompt: Implement the tasks from epic ARC-5. Start with ARC-5.1 (JWT token generation).

assertions.text: Output dispatches an arc-implementer subagent via Agent tool
Output does NOT write implementation code directly (no Write/Edit tool calls for .go/.ts files)
Subagent dispatch prompt includes the task spec from `arc show`
Subagent dispatch prompt includes the project test command

prompt: Start implementing the next ready task.

assertions.text: Output runs `arc ready` to find next task
Output runs `arc update <id> --status in_progress` before dispatching subagent
Output records PRE_TASK_SHA before dispatching

eval_name: Implement verifies result and dispatches evaluation + review in parallel
prompt: The arc-implementer just reported back with PASS on all gate checks for ARC-5.1. This task is a child of epic ARC-5.

assertions.text: Output runs the project test command fresh (does NOT trust subagent report alone)
Output dispatches arc-evaluator AND arc-reviewer (via review skill) simultaneously after tests pass
Evaluator dispatch prompt includes the task spec and design spec but NOT the git diff
Both agent dispatches happen in a single response (parallel, not sequential)
Output closes the task with `arc close` only after independent test verification, evaluation, AND review all pass

prompt: The arc-implementer reported PARTIAL. Gate: Unresolved — spec compliance missed step 3 (error handling for invalid tokens). Test coverage missing edge case for expired tokens.

assertions.text: Output does NOT close the task
Output re-dispatches arc-implementer with the specific gate feedback included in the prompt
Re-dispatch prompt includes the original task spec AND the previous gate feedback

eval_name: Implement routes docs-only tasks to doc-writer
prompt: The next ready task ARC-5.4 has a docs-only label. It's about updating the API documentation.

assertions.text: Output checks for the docs-only label (via arc show --json + jq or similar)
Output dispatches arc-doc-writer subagent (NOT arc-implementer)

eval_name: Implement parallel dispatch uses worktree isolation
prompt: Tasks ARC-5.2, ARC-5.3, and ARC-5.4 are independent with no shared files. Dispatch them in parallel.

assertions.text: Output commits and pushes all sequential work before parallel dispatch
Output records PARALLEL_BASE SHA before dispatching
All parallel Agent calls use isolation: 'worktree'
All parallel Agent calls are in a single message (not spread across multiple turns)
After agents complete, output verifies commit history against PARALLEL_BASE

eval_name: Implement runs integration checkpoint after 2-3 tasks
prompt: We just closed ARC-5.1 and ARC-5.2. ARC-5.3 is next.

assertions.text: Output runs integration tests (make test-integration or equivalent) before starting ARC-5.3

eval_name: Implement handles deviation from review — fix path
prompt: The review skill reported a Deviation (fix) for ARC-5.1: the implementer used string IDs but the design specifies int64. The reviewer recommends fixing to match the design.

assertions.text: Output re-dispatches arc-implementer with explicit instruction to change string IDs to int64
Output does NOT close the task until the deviation is resolved
Output re-dispatches review after the fix to confirm the deviation is resolved

eval_name: Implement handles deviation from review — accept path
prompt: The review skill reported a Deviation (accept) for ARC-5.3: the implementer used a custom error type instead of the plain error return specified in the design. The reviewer says the deviation aligns with existing codebase conventions.

assertions.text: Output accepts the deviation rather than forcing a fix
Output logs the accepted deviation as an arc comment on the task with description and rationale
Output proceeds to close the task (does not re-dispatch for accepted deviations)

eval_name: Implement handles evaluator FAIL — spec-intent gap
prompt: The arc-evaluator reported FAIL for ARC-5.2. Spec-Intent Gap: spec says 'Return 401 if refresh token is expired' but the evaluator's acceptance test shows the endpoint returns 400 instead of 401 for expired tokens.

assertions.text: Output re-dispatches arc-implementer with the evaluator's finding (expected 401, got 400 for expired tokens)
Output does NOT close the task while the spec-intent gap is unresolved
Output re-dispatches evaluator after the fix to verify spec compliance (not just reviewer)

eval_name: Implement prioritizes evaluator spec-intent over reviewer code quality
prompt: For ARC-5.3, the arc-reviewer says the code is clean with no issues. But the arc-evaluator reports FAIL: spec requires 'idempotent delete — deleting a non-existent resource returns 204' but the acceptance test shows 404 is returned.

assertions.text: Output treats the evaluator's spec-intent finding as the priority issue to fix
Output re-dispatches arc-implementer to fix the idempotent delete behavior (204 not 404)
Output does NOT close the task because the reviewer said 'no issues' — the evaluator's FAIL takes precedence

eval_name: Implement applies evaluator circuit breaker after 3 cycles
prompt: This is the 3rd evaluate/fix cycle for ARC-5.4. The evaluator keeps reporting FAIL on the same finding: spec says 'rate limit per user' but the implementation rate-limits per IP. The implementer keeps re-implementing per-IP rate limiting.

assertions.text: Output recognizes the 3-cycle evaluator circuit breaker threshold
Output STOPS the evaluate/fix loop and escalates to the user
Output presents both interpretations (evaluator: per-user, implementer: per-IP) for the user to clarify
Output does NOT dispatch a 4th cycle without user guidance

eval_name: Implement skips evaluator for docs-only tasks
prompt: The arc-doc-writer just completed ARC-5.9, a docs-only task for updating API reference docs.

assertions.text: Output does NOT dispatch arc-evaluator for docs-only tasks
Output proceeds to close the task without adversarial evaluation

eval_name: Implement handles evaluator BLOCKED — does not blame implementer
prompt: The arc-evaluator reported BLOCKED for ARC-5.6. Implementation Health shows PASS (project builds, tests pass). Evaluator Setup shows FAIL (acceptance tests couldn't compile due to a dependency conflict the evaluator introduced).

assertions.text: Output does NOT re-dispatch arc-implementer — BLOCKED is the evaluator's problem, not the implementer's
Output notes the inconclusive evaluation in an arc comment
Output proceeds with the workflow (review findings are still valid even if evaluator was BLOCKED)

eval_name: Implement handles evaluator FAIL due to broken implementer build
prompt: The arc-evaluator reported FAIL for ARC-5.7. Implementation Health shows FAIL — the project doesn't compile. The evaluator never got to run acceptance tests because the build itself is broken.

assertions.text: Output re-dispatches arc-implementer with the build failure as a Critical finding to fix
Output does NOT treat this as an evaluator problem — a broken build is the implementer's fault
Output does NOT close the task while the build is broken

eval_name: Implement dispatches evaluator with worktree isolation
prompt: The arc-implementer just reported PASS on all gate checks for ARC-5.3. Tests pass. Time to dispatch evaluation and review.

assertions.text: Evaluator agent is dispatched with isolation: 'worktree'
Reviewer agent is NOT dispatched with worktree isolation (it is read-only and doesn't need it)
